sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.65 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: (Well-known problem). For a finite group$G$let$(G)$denote the set of prime numbers dividing the order of some conjugacy class, and$(G)$the maximum number of primes dividing the order of some conjugacy class. Is it true that$| (G)| 3 (G)$? A possible linear bound for$| (G)|$in terms of$(G)$cannot be better than$3 (G)$, since there is a family of groups$\{G_n\}$such that$_{n }| (G_n)|/ (G_n)=3$(C.\,...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
